phrases.french: Les couleurs des façades sont incroyables !
phrases.english: The colours of the facades are incredible!
phrases.phonetic: lay koo-LUR day fah-SAHD son an-krwah-YAH-bluh
phrases.context: Menton's old town is famous for its pastel-coloured buildings — ochre, coral, yellow and terracotta. Italians call this style Ligurian baroque.

phrases.french: Est-ce que je peux me promener dans les ruelles ?
phrases.english: Can I walk around the narrow streets?
phrases.phonetic: es-kuh zhuh puh muh proh-muh-NAY don lay roo-EL
phrases.context: The old town is entirely pedestrianised and free to explore. The steep lanes (ruelles) lead up to the basilica and offer sea views.

phrases.french: Où est le cimetière du Vieux Château ?
phrases.english: Where is the Old Castle Cemetery?
phrases.phonetic: oo eh luh seem-TYAIR dew vyuh sha-TOH
phrases.context: The cemetery at the top of the old town has extraordinary panoramic views and is the burial site of many European aristocrats who came to Menton for the climate.

tips: Menton's old town was part of the Principality of Monaco until 1848 — the Italian influence shows in every street
The Old Castle Cemetery at the top is free and has one of the best panoramic views of the Riviera
The Salle des Mariages in the town hall was decorated by Jean Cocteau — visit for a small fee
